mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate remains in the zero lower bound regime.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.11% as of 2015-01-15 indicates sustained accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 200k signals robust labor market tightening that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings. Positive employment momentum drives positive equity returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance improves.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between PAYEMS monthly change and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.10 over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth deceleration combined with housing starts (HOUST) decline signals weakening aggregate demand. When both series show negative momentum simultaneously, equity valuations compress due to lower expected earnings growth, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth < 2% AND HOUST 3-month average < 1050k units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 439771M and HOUST at 1089k as of 2015-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite dual demand weakness, or the interaction effect (RSAFS × HOUST) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction signals supply-side weakness and potential deflationary pressure. When INDPRO declines for two consecutive months, equity markets reprrice earnings downward, producing negative 60-day forward returns as the market anticipates recession risk.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) acceleration above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 0.5%) signals monetary tightening cycle that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This interaction mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns when both conditions are met.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 236.149 and FEDFUNDS at 0.11% as of 2015-01-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite tightening signals, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) shows Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) decline below 5.5% signals tight labor market and wage pressure that supports consumer income and equity valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns when unemployment is in the sub-5.5% regime, reflecting full employment conditions.
regime_hint: tight labor market approaching full employment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of observations when UNRATE < 5.5%, or Rank IC between UNRATE and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration above 2.5% annualized signals strong economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations. When quarterly GDP growth exceeds trend, equity markets reprrice upward, producing positive 60-day forward returns as forward guidance improves.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals supply-demand balance tightening that supports equity valuations through both earnings growth and inflation expectations. This interaction mechanism predicts positive 5-day returns when both labor and supply conditions strengthen simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.5114 and UNRATE at 5.7% as of 2015-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite dual tightening signals, or the interaction term (INDPRO growth × UNRATE decline) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
